item.title: Clearway Energy's solar deal points to derisked renewables push
item.description: Clearway Energy's acquisition of Deriva's solar-plus-storage portfolio reflects wider shift towards operating assets to meet hyperscaler demand as Trump risk threatens power capacity ramp

item.title: Mingyang gets go-ahead for colossal wind and green hydrogen project
item.description: The Chinese wind turbine giant had originally signed a $10bn investment agreement, but has now boosted its planned spending

item.title: Three best European markets to invest in co-located renewables and battery storage
item.description: Paring storage with renewables will be key to reduce costs and grid constraints, report points out

item.title: The scale of Big Oil's retreat from wind and solar – and why it's speeding up
item.description: Major players all cut clean energy spending even before impact of latest crisis tilts them further towards core segments, says BNEF

item.title: Siemens Energy CEO: Offshore wind projects not getting FIDs fast enough
item.description: All projects auctioned off at lower interest rates years ago now face challenges as rates have risen, Christian Bruch said

item.title: Siemens Gamesa 'on track to break even' as first 7MW orders trickle in
item.description: Parent Siemens Energy says wind turbine unit’s loss narrows driven by onshore news units business

item.title: Data centres could trigger new ‘Spain-style’ blackouts and force grids to curb wind: Report
item.description: A high concentration of data centres could create local voltage problems that develop into frequency disturbances across the wider power system, according to a new report from the European transmission system operators, Entso-E

item.title: Crown Estate unveils fresh £15m boost for offshore wind supply chains
item.description: The investment is part of a push to strengthen domestic clean energy manufacturing amid growing concerns of dependence on international supply

item.title: Chinese giant plans Canada entry with large wind-storage project
item.description: Canada continues its trajectory to add wind power as the US backs away

item.title: Chinese giant plans Canada entry with large wind-storage project
item.description: Canada continues its trajectory to add wind power as the US backs away

item.title: Iran oil crisis: geography of energy risk is changing with renewables, not disappearing
item.description: Despite the energy independence promised by batteries, the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint shows risks of overconcentrating green tech supply in one country

item.title: Enercon CEO expects only indirect impact from Iran war on business
item.description: German wind turbine maker has no business in affected countries and does not source materials from the region, so crisis not likely to have direct effect, CEO Udo Bauer tells Recharge

item.title: Suzlon eyes building new wind turbine factory in Europe
item.description: Indian wind turbine giant is going through a major strategy revamp as it pushes into new markets and technologies, including solar panels and battery storage

item.title: Ten years of Nordex-Acciona merger – a lesson in empire building
item.description: Acciona boss Entrecanales supported Nordex in good and bad times, and boosted the German wind turbine manufacturer’s size and worth – more may be on the horizon

item.title: A masterstroke of empire-building: How Acciona turned Nordex from takeover target into European wind merger player
item.description: Acciona boss Entrecanales supported Nordex in good and bad times, and boosted the German wind turbine manufacturer’s size and worth – more may be on the horizon

item.title: Total faces divestment threat over US offshore wind retreat
item.description: New York State comptroller highlights legal and regulatory risks posed by Trump lease buyout agreement

item.title: Deals of the Week: Octopus, Vestas and Masdar
item.description: Two renewable energy developers make plays for European wind market, while Danish wind turbine giant Vestas announces trio of deals

item.title: Europe's offshore wind turbine prices have soared as supplier choice dwindled
item.description: Siemens Gamesa and Vestas now control virtually all nacelle and blade supply to European developers, study points out

item.title: Why Iberdrola's push for price independence now runs through the UK
item.description: Networks delivered all of Iberdrola’s growth, while the UK delivered most of the growth in networks.

item.title: 'In no way normal': Legal experts question Trump offshore wind buyouts
item.description: Former Department of Interior leaders and lawyers highlight violations by administration and developers

item.title: Germany opens €5bn subsidy scheme to help industry electrify
item.description: Firms can bid for 15-year Carbon Contracts for Difference

item.title: Engie CEO says batteries making wind and solar bankable
item.description: Battery storage with renewables presents 'fantastic opportunities' says French group's boss

item.title: SMRs v renewables: mini nuclear's costs judgement day is coming
item.description: The EU is the latest to give its stamp of approval to new, smaller scale nuclear technology – but experts say the big costs test against wind and solar power is yet to come

item.title: Reform hits back against RenewableUK chief in row over wind farm support
item.description: Reform UK has pledged to scrap Contracts for Difference for wind and solar farms should it win power, prompting warnings this would undermine investor confidence in the country

item.title: EU chief: 'we've been too naive for too long' over Chinese danger in wind and solar
item.description: Climate chief Wopke Hoekstra delivers hawkish message as WindEurope CEO claims UK shows the way with Mingyang exclusion

item.title: Mingyang profit collapses despite record wind turbine sales
item.description: Record turbine sales and improving product margins point to a wind recovery, but a core loss, dividend cut and weaker power-station returns show Mingyang has yet to turn volume into durable earnings.

item.title: US wind power leader pivots to gas in $78bn buildout
item.description: AEP added over 1GW of wind in 2025, but its plans focus on fossil fuel generation to meet 63GW of demand

item.title: Grid delays slow Orsted's offshore wind projects in two core markets
item.description: Orsted faces a two-month delay to the onshore grid connection for Hornsea 3, but CEO sees 'manageable' risk

item.title: Vestas CFO doesn't expect offshore wind to be profitable this year
item.description: Wegge-Larsen also tells Recharge that business of wind turbine giant has so far escaped unscathed from Trump administration attacks on US permitting and Iran war

item.title: US impairments dent Orsted profit in windy quarter
item.description: Orsted's underlying earnings bounced back in the first quarter, but tax and impairment adjustments in the US took a bite out of income
